name: vscode-extension-host
query: How does the extension host communicate with the main process?

requiredClaims.claim: The extension host and main side communicate over an RPC/protocol layer.
Extension-host services expose ExtHost-side objects and main-side counterparts.
Communication uses serialized messages over an IPC channel or message port.
The bootstrap creates the extension host process/worker and wires protocol connections.

name: excalidraw-render-update
query: How does Excalidraw render and update canvas elements?

requiredClaims.claim: Scene or app state changes schedule a render/update of the canvas.
Elements are rendered through a canvas renderer that iterates visible elements.
RoughJS or shape drawing utilities render individual element types.
Interactive updates are driven by pointer/event handlers mutating elements/app state.

name: django-queryset-execution
query: How does Django's ORM build and execute a query from a QuerySet?

requiredClaims.claim: QuerySet methods clone and refine a Query object lazily.
Filters and other operations add constraints to the internal Query.
Evaluation triggers fetching results and uses an iterable/cache.
SQLCompiler compiles the Query to SQL and executes it through the database connection/cursor.

name: tokio-runtime-scheduling
query: How does tokio schedule and run async tasks on its runtime?

requiredClaims.claim: Runtime/Handle spawn creates tasks and injects them into a scheduler.
Tasks are represented as task/harness/raw task structures that poll futures.
The multi-thread scheduler uses worker queues, injection/global queues, and stealing.
Wakers notify or schedule tasks back onto the runtime.

name: okhttp-interceptor-chain
query: How does OkHttp process a request through its interceptor chain?

requiredClaims.claim: RealCall builds an ordered interceptor list.
RealInterceptorChain carries the request and index through proceed calls.
Application interceptors run before core/network interceptors.
The final server interceptor writes the request and reads the response.

name: gin-middleware-routing
query: Trace Gin request routing end to end: how routes and middleware are registered, how method trees match a request path, how the matched handlers are assigned and executed through Context.Next, and how 404/405 fallback handlers reuse the same chain mechanism.

requiredClaims.claim: Engine.ServeHTTP obtains a Context and dispatches to handleHTTPRequest.
Routes are stored in method trees and matched by HTTP method/path.
RouterGroup combines middleware handlers with endpoint handlers.
Context.Next advances through handlers by index.
After route match, Gin assigns handlers to the context and runs c.Next.
404 and 405 fallback handlers are combined with middleware and executed through the same c.handlers and c.Next mechanism.

name: alamofire-request-lifecycle
query: How does Alamofire build, send, and validate a request?

requiredClaims.claim: Session creates Request subclasses from URLRequestConvertible/URLConvertible inputs.
Request creation and execution are coordinated through SessionDelegate or underlying URLSession tasks.
RequestAdapter/RequestRetrier or interceptor can adapt and retry requests.
Validation and response serialization run after receiving responses/data.
